description: A tightly knit agrarian community of 500 souls clinging to survival amidst a severe, unrelenting winter. Social cohesion is the primary defense against starvation and disease, enforced by a rigid hierarchy and shared rationing protocols.

Lord/Lady.description: The ruling family managing overall resource allocation, mediating disputes, and making life-or-death decisions on who receives extra rations.

Steward/Overseer.description: Administrators responsible for inventory, enforcing rationing laws, and reporting directly to the Lord. They bear the brunt of public anger.

Healer/Wise Woman.description: Overworked herbalists and midwives treating frostbite, plague, and malnutrition with limited supplies. Often the target of superstition or blame.

Blacksmith/Fletcher.description: Critical artisans maintaining tools, repairing roofs, and crafting weapons against wolves. Their work keeps the settlement functional.

Hunter/Trapper.description: Risky role venturing into deep snow for meat and fur. High mortality rate due to cold and predators; success is the only buffer against starvation.

Farmer/Forager.description: The backbone of the village, tasked with maintaining the communal fire, guarding grain stores, and gathering scarce wood and dried roots.

Elder/Storyteller.description: Respected figures who preserve history, teach survival skills, and provide psychological comfort through oral tradition during long nights.

Child/Adolescent.description: Assigned light labor like fetching water, mending nets, or caring for younger siblings. Their health is the first to suffer from malnutrition.

Sick/Infirmed.description: Those bedridden by the 'Winter Pox' or severe frostbite. They are dependent on communal care and face the highest risk of being abandoned.

daily_rhythms.04:00: Dawn prayers and immediate check of the communal fire; the sick are warmed first.
daily_rhythms.05:00: Stewards distribute the day's grain ration (strictly measured). Hunters depart before full light.
daily_rhythms.06:00: Labor begins: farmers check grain silos for rot, blacksmiths tend the forge, women mend clothes.
daily_rhythms.12:00: Midday meal: A thin broth shared in the Great Hall. Brief respite to conserve body heat.
daily_rhythms.14:00: Afternoon labor continues; children are sent to gather firewood or tend to livestock.
daily_rhythms.16:00: Work stops as light fades. Everyone retreats indoors to conserve heat.
daily_rhythms.17:00: Evening meal: The heaviest meal of the day, often just more broth or a piece of dried meat.
daily_rhythms.18:00: Community gathering in the Great Hall for storytelling, dispute resolution, or planning.
daily_rhythms.20:00: Lights (candles/oil) are extinguished to save fuel. Families huddle together for warmth.
daily_rhythms.23:00: Silence falls; only the watchmen and the dying remain awake.

recurring_events.name: The Ration Count
recurring_events.description: Stewards publicly weigh and distribute grain. Anyone caught hoarding is publicly shamed or punished.

recurring_events.name: The Healing Vigil
recurring_events.description: The entire village gathers to pray for the sick and share herbal remedies. A time of collective grief and hope.

recurring_events.name: The Council of Elders
recurring_events.description: Leadership meets to decide on resource reallocation and potential sacrifices (e.g., who gets the last medicine).

recurring_events.name: The Bone-Burn
recurring_events.description: The dead are cremated or buried in frozen ground to prevent disease spread. A somber ritual where names are called.

random_events.name: The Plague Breach
random_events.description: A new strain of fever spreads rapidly through the crowded housing, causing panic and a spike in mortality.

random_events.name: The Wolf Pack Raid
random_events.description: Starving wolves breach the outer palisade, killing livestock and injuring a hunter.

random_events.name: The Hoard Discovery
random_events.description: A hidden cache of rot-resistant food is found, sparking a temporary celebration or a violent conflict over ownership.

random_events.name: The Snowslide
random_events.description: Heavy snow collapses a section of the village, trapping families under the debris.

Great Hall.description: The central heated building where meals are eaten, disputes settled, and the sick are sheltered. The heart of community bonding.

Communal Granary.description: A heavily guarded, insulated storehouse for the village's food supply. Access is strictly controlled.

Hut Clusters.description: Small, single-room dwellings packed tightly for warmth. Often overcrowded, leading to rapid disease spread.

The Infirmary.description: A drafty but designated space for the sick. Often viewed with fear and avoided by the healthy.

The Smithy.description: The only consistently warm places in the village due to the forge, used for essential repairs.

Watchtowers.description: Elevated platforms for spotting predators and monitoring the perimeter.

groups: The Kinship Clans (Extended families living in clusters)
social_structures.interaction_patterns: Interactions are highly transactional regarding resources but deeply emotional regarding kinship. Trust is low for outsiders but high within families. Public shaming is a common social control mechanism. Silence is respected as a sign of mourning or respect.

stressors: Rapid spread of 'Winter Pox' and frostbite
Extreme cold and lack of fuel
Fear of resource hoarding by neighbors
Loss of family members to disease or cold
support_systems: Shared meals in the Great Hall
Religious/ritualistic prayers for the dead and sick
The authority of Elders providing wisdom and calm
The forge (physical warmth and gathering point)
